summary: Device Health Rule Management endpoints testing complete. All 23 backend API tests passed (100%). All features verified: GET list all rules, PUT update rules with validation, PATCH toggle enabled/disabled, RBAC enforcement (operator/admin only), threshold key validation, cache invalidation.
critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/operator/health-rules returns all 3 rules (low_battery, signal_degradation, reboot_anomaly)
Response contains exactly canonical fields: rule_name, enabled, threshold_json, cooldown_minutes, severity, updated_at
No 'id' field leaked in response (internal UUIDs not exposed)
PUT validates threshold_json keys match rule schema (unknown keys rejected, missing keys rejected)
RBAC enforced: operator/admin allowed, guardian gets 403, unauthenticated gets 401
Cache invalidation works: changes reflected immediately in GET after PUT/PATCH

Each rule has specific required threshold keys: low_battery={battery_percent, sustain_minutes, recovery_buffer}, signal_degradation={signal_threshold, sustain_minutes, recovery_buffer_dbm}, reboot_anomaly={gap_minutes, gap_count, window_minutes}
seed_data_creation: None - tests use existing rules (low_battery, signal_degradation, reboot_anomaly). All rules restored to original values after tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Health Rule Management endpoints fully functional. Three rules exist: low_battery, signal_degradation, reboot_anomaly. Each has specific threshold_json keys. GET /api/operator/health-rules lists all. PUT /api/operator/health-rules/{rule_name} updates with validation. PATCH /api/operator/health-rules/{rule_name}/toggle toggles enabled state. All endpoints require operator/admin role. Original values: low_battery (battery_percent=20, sustain_minutes=10, recovery_buffer=5, cooldown=60, severity=low), signal_degradation (signal_threshold=-80, sustain_minutes=10, recovery_buffer_dbm=5, cooldown=60, severity=low), reboot_anomaly (gap_minutes=3, gap_count=3, window_minutes=60, cooldown=120, severity=medium).

backend_api_tests: GET /api/operator/health-rules returns 200 with 3 rules
Response has canonical fields only (rule_name, enabled, threshold_json, cooldown_minutes, severity, updated_at)
No 'id' field in response (no internal IDs leaked)
GET without auth returns 401
GET with guardian token returns 403
PUT /api/operator/health-rules/low_battery with valid payload returns 200
PUT with invalid severity='critical' returns 422
PUT with unknown threshold key returns 422
PUT with missing required threshold key returns 422
PUT to nonexistent_rule returns 404
PUT with guardian token returns 403
PUT signal_degradation with valid threshold_json updates correctly
PUT reboot_anomaly with valid threshold_json updates correctly
PATCH toggle with enabled=false disables rule
PATCH toggle with enabled=true enables rule
PATCH toggle on fake_rule returns 404
PATCH toggle with guardian token returns 403
Cache invalidation after PUT (GET reflects changes)
Cache invalidation after toggle (GET reflects changes)
Partial update preserves unspecified fields
All valid severities (low, medium, high) accepted
signal_degradation rejects wrong threshold keys (422)
reboot_anomaly rejects wrong threshold keys (422)

verified_features: GET /api/operator/health-rules returns all 3 rules with canonical response
PUT /api/operator/health-rules/low_battery with valid full payload updates and returns canonical response with updated_at
PUT /api/operator/health-rules/low_battery with unknown threshold key returns 422
PUT /api/operator/health-rules/low_battery with missing required threshold key returns 422
PATCH /api/operator/health-rules/low_battery/toggle with {enabled: false} returns canonical response with enabled=false
PUT /api/operator/health-rules/signal_degradation with valid threshold_json updates correctly
PUT /api/operator/health-rules/reboot_anomaly with valid threshold_json updates correctly
Response never contains 'id' field (no internal IDs leaked)
After PUT update, GET /api/operator/health-rules reflects the change (cache invalidation works)
